drop file: Click here to select file or drop a file right here
auto_format_desc: Try to auto format even when the text is not a valid XML while pasting text or drag-and-dropping a file into the editor.
auto_sort_desc: Sort XML data in ascending order when pasting text or drag-and-drop a file into the editor.
nested_parse_desc: Recursively parse the string fields in XML into XML object and output the whole as a new XML.
sync_reveal_desc: Sync scrolling the left editor and the right editor when viewing the compare result. Sync the cursor position in the editor and the highlighting of the node in the graph view.
share_desc: Anyone who has this link will be able to view this (Link valid for 7 days).
jq_disabled: Used up this month's jq execution quota
swap_left_right: Swap left and right text (shortcut: ⌘ Enter or ^ Enter)
convert to XML: File will be converted to XML
CsvErrMissingQuotes: Parse CSV failed: missing paired quotes
CsvErrTooFewFields: Parse CSV failed: inconsistent number of fields, some rows have fewer fields
CsvErrTooManyFields: Parse CSV failed: inconsistent number of fields, some rows have more fields
CsvErrEmptyInput: Parse CSV failed: input is empty
CsvCvrtErrNotArray: Convert to CSV failed: not a XML array
CsvCvrtErrNotMatchHeader: Convert to CSV failed: headers are not match with data
double_click_to_reveal_first_child: Double-click to focus on the first child node

Home.landing_subtitle: Boost efficiency with all-in-one XML toolkit designed for you – visualize, format, and compare your data like never before!
Home.Visualize_description: Visualizing your data using graph or table with interactive features (e.g. quick location via xml path).
Home.Compare_description: Both structured compare and text compare are available to quickly find the differences between two XMLs.
Home.Command_description: A number of commands are available through the quick search bar for you to process your XML.
Home.Format_description: Fast formatting when importing file or pasting data. Intelligent formatting for non-XML text.
Home.Validate_description: Validate with context information to help you find and location the error quickly.

Pricing.title: Pricing
Pricing.free_desc: No need to bill but with usage limitations.
Pricing.monthly_desc: Billed monthly with no usage limitations.
Pricing.yearly_desc: Annually bill of $60 with no usage limitations.
Pricing.no_pricing: Currently free, but in the future, there will be charges for some unique features based on usage.

FAQ.a_why_build1: Most similar sites only support the most common validate, format, minify, escape, unescape and tree view features, a small number of sites provide a structured comparison. But few sites provide BigInt comparison, text comparison, in-line comparison, array difference comparison, so I made XML For You to address it.
FAQ.a_why_build2: After two years of use, I found that XML For You is good enough to process XML data, but it is not convenient to view XML data. For example, when encountering a complex XML structure, XML For You can quickly view the formatted text, but only the text form can't let developers quickly understand the XML structure. Therefore, I have launched XML For You 3.0.0, which provides two view modes: graph view and table view. For complex object type data, you can quickly understand it through the graph view, and for complex array type data, you can quickly understand it through the table view.
FAQ.q_support_features: What features does XML For You support?
FAQ.a_support_features1: XML Validation - Automatically validates when modified. Error lines will be marked, and sufficient context will be provided to help locate errors.
FAQ.a_support_features2: XML Formatting - Automatically formats when dragging files or pasting, even if the XML data is invalid.
FAQ.a_support_features3: XML Minimization - Removes all whitespace characters, compressing the XML data into a single line.
FAQ.a_support_features4: XML Escaping - Escapes XML data by adding \ characters to make it a valid string.
FAQ.a_support_features5: XML Unescaping - Unescapes XML data by removing \ characters to make it valid XML.
FAQ.a_support_features6: XML Sorting - Recursively sorts keys but does not sort arrays; the XML before and after sorting is semantically equal. Commonly used for comparison after sorting, allowing diffs to be grouped together for easier visual inspection.
FAQ.a_support_features7: Display XML Path - Clicking anywhere will show the corresponding token's XML Pointer. Used in conjunction with the minimap, it helps quickly understand the structure of the XML.
FAQ.a_support_features8: CSV to XML - Converts CSV files to XML, supporting both header and no-header cases.
FAQ.a_support_features9: XML to CSV - Converts XML arrays into CSV files.
FAQ.a_support_features10: URL to XML - Recursively parses a URL into XML. If you need to compare the diff of two URLs, converting them to XML first makes it very convenient.
FAQ.a_support_features11: BigInt/int64 Comparison - In business development, IDs are often defined as int64, while JS's number can only accurately represent 52-bit integers. Without special handling on the front and back ends, precision can be lost. To quickly identify such differences, BigInt comparison functionality is supported.
FAQ.a_support_features12: Word-by-Word Comparison - Sometimes two XML data sets have only minor differences, such as two long strings that differ by only one or two words, making it difficult to visually identify the specific inconsistencies. Therefore, word-by-word comparison is supported, allowing you to easily spot the exact diffs.
FAQ.a_support_features13: Array Diff Comparison - If two XML arrays have differences, we generally do not need to view the diff of every single element, as there may be a lot of noise that is inconvenient for visual inspection. Therefore, a diff comparison is provided, offering a concise, human-readable difference similar to git diff.
FAQ.a_support_features14: Text Comparison - Since text comparison is also a common requirement, support has been added to downgrade invalid XML to text comparison (text diff), providing an experience similar to git diff.
FAQ.q_what_is_jq: What is jq and how to use it in XML For You?
FAQ.a_what_is_jq: <jq>jq</jq> is like sed for XML data - you can use it to slice and filter and map and transform structured data with the same ease that sed, awk, grep and friends let you play with text. XML For You support to use jq online (version 1.7), you can input "jq" in the quick command bar to use it.
FAQ.q_advantages: What are the differences from other XML websites?

Metadata.title: XML For You | The best online XML tool
Metadata.keywords: xml viewer, xml formatter, xml beautifier, pretty xml, xml validate, xml compare
Metadata.description: Online XML view, format, minify, validate and compare. It also provides graph view to preview XML data, semantic comparing two XML diffs, convert XML to CSV or CSV to XML and support jq online.
